title: Cookies, Credential Modes, API Keys, Bearer Tokens, and Secret Hygiene · Browser and Node Client… · Inference Brief: Ambient Authority Csrf
summary: A browser client selects omit, same-origin, or include credentials and separates cookies, CSRF, API keys, and bearer-token authority. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts ambient authority csrf increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch least-authority credential and session policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Recognize ambient cookie authority and require CSRF defenses for state-changing browser requests. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Apply cookie domain, host-only, path, expiry, Secure, HttpOnly, and SameSite scope through a deliberate jar policy. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Fetch and TypeScript Predict Ambient Authority Csrf · Theory Ambient Authority Csrf
steps.instruction:
theory case — TypeScript Fetch evidence model handles host-only session cookie SID-5, SameSite policy, API key, and expiring bearer token while two accounts run concurrently. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
